market_summary: S&P 500 +0.8% to 7,165 (record close). Nasdaq +1.63% to 24,837 (record). Dow -0.16% to 49,231. Risk-on driven by US-Iran peace talks (FM Araghchi heading to Pakistan) and Intel AI earnings momentum. Semiconductor rally: NVDA crossed $5T again, AMD +13%, QCOM +10%. BUT: Consumer sentiment at record low (49.8). Oil surged 13-16% weekly (Strait of Hormuz crisis since Feb 28). Gold above $4,800. Crypto steady: BTC $78K, ETH $2,312.

key_events_next_week.note: Rate decision + statement Wed afternoon. Critical for all positions.

overnight_risks: US-Iran negotiations in Pakistan — ceasefire or escalation could move oil 5-10% either way
Strait of Hormuz still largely blocked since Feb 28. Largest oil supply disruption in history (-10.1 mb/d in March)
Asia-Pacific open: watching for reaction to US record closes and oil volatility
FOMC meeting Tue-Wed: market positioning ahead of rate decision could trigger pre-meeting volatility
Weekend weekend risk: geopolitical headlines could gap markets at Sunday crypto open

instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver at $75.7, consolidating after $80+ test. Broke 13-year $50-54 resistance zone earlier in 2026. JPM gold target $6,300 supports silver rally thesis. Gold-silver ratio compressed from 90 to ~62. Structural bull intact but short-term extended.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold surpassed $4,805 on Apr 20, consolidating around $4,710. JPMorgan raised target to $6,300/oz by end 2026. Central banks buying 585 tonnes/quarter. FOMC decision this week is key — hawkish hold could pressure metals short-term.
instrument_news.BTC-USD: BTC at $78K, up 5.81% in 5 days. Range-bound $76K-$80K. Bitcoin dominance near 59%. Institutional flows steady. FOMC and macro data this week will determine if breakout above $80K happens.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: ETH at $2,312, up 2.73% in 5 days. Grayscale/Bitmine staked nearly $500M in ETH on Apr 25. US spot ETH ETFs saw $23.4M net inflows, reversing outflow trend. Still underperforming BTC.
instrument_news.MSTR: MSTR at $171.02 (-0.8% from prev close $172.47). Now holds 815,061 BTC (surpassed BlackRock's Bitcoin Trust). Massive EPS miss last quarter (-$42.93 vs $2.97 forecast). Stock down 53.62% over 1 year. Heavily correlated to BTC — watch $80K BTC level.

metals_outlook: Structurally bullish. Gold near record $4,800+, central banks accumulating 585t/quarter. JPM target $6,300 EOY. Silver broke multi-decade resistance. BUT: FOMC this week could trigger short-term hawkish selloff if Fed signals higher-for-longer. Oil-driven inflation (Brent $105) complicates Fed narrative — bullish for gold as inflation hedge, but hawkish policy response could temporarily pressure all commodities.
oil_outlook: Brent $105, WTI $94. Weekly gains 13-16%. Strait of Hormuz blockade since Feb 28 is the dominant driver — largest supply disruption in history. US-Iran peace talks in Pakistan could ease tensions. Dual risk: escalation → oil spikes to $120+; breakthrough → oil drops 15-20% rapidly. Oil volatility is the single biggest macro variable right now.
sentiment_shift: Equities euphoric (record highs, semiconductor mania), consumer sentiment at record lows (49.8). Classic divergence — market optimism vs real economy pessimism. Oil-driven inflation squeezing consumers while tech AI narrative lifts stocks. This divergence historically resolves violently.
fomc_preview: FOMC Apr 28-29 is THE event. Oil-driven CPI/PCE inflation argues against cuts. But consumer sentiment at record low and geopolitical risk argue for caution. Most likely: hawkish hold, no cut, acknowledge oil-driven inflation. Market reaction depends on language — any hint of cuts = metals/crypto rally, any hawkish surprise = sell-off across risk assets.
